title: Automated vegetation monitoring across a national network
summary: CLMS-derived canopy-change detection replaced manual line-side inspection across thousands of kilometres of rural corridor.
narrative:
## Challenge
Monitoring vegetation encroachment along 8,000 km of rural track via manual patrols was expensive, seasonal and inconsistent, contributing to recurring overhead-line contact incidents.

## Approach
Annual CLMS tree-cover-density and vegetation-phenology products from Sentinel-2 were processed to detect canopy expansion within a corridor buffer. Change alerts above a density threshold triggered targeted inspection work orders.

## Results
In the first year the system produced several hundred encroachment alerts, the large majority confirmed actionable on field verification. Reactive vegetation incidents fell substantially over two years, with significant annual inspection-cost savings.
